patch.value: Extension API missing self-identity accessor: extensions cannot read their own name, layer, or version at activation

The ExtensionApi interface (extension-types.ts line 754) exposes only register* methods and hooks.*. An extension's activate(api) function has no way to read its own manifest metadata (name, version, layer) from the api object. Authors must either embed it redundantly in-module or access the file-system manifest. A read-only `api.manifest` or `api.extension` property (name, version, layer, capabilities, pm_min_version) would allow extensions to emit self-identifying logs, gate on their own version, and produce better error messages without duplicating manifest data.

- Extension `activate(api)` exposes the extension's own name, layer, and version (e.g. api.extension)
- Available at activation without re-reading the manifest
- SDK types + docs updated; covered by a test

author: maintainer-agent
message: Bundled into Extension SDK platform-maturity PR (epic pm-ugqx)

patch.value: ExtensionApi now exposes read-only frozen api.extension self-identity (name/layer/version/capabilities/pm_min_version/pm_max_version/source_package) built in createExtensionApi. Documented + tested.
